category.label: REGULATORS
category.description: Who makes the rules, what the rules are, and why they matter — the regulatory framework every trader operates inside.

entries.title: SEC — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
entries.subtitle: Primary regulator of U.S. securities markets: disclosure rules, insider trading enforcement, broker-dealer oversight.

rules_of_thumb.label: 8-K filing deadline
rules_of_thumb.note: Companies must file 8-K for material events within 4 business days

rules_of_thumb.label: 13F threshold
rules_of_thumb.note: Institutional managers above this must disclose equity holdings quarterly

rules_of_thumb.label: 13D filing trigger
rules_of_thumb.note: Must file within 10 days of crossing 5% in any class

pitfalls: Tipping MNPI to others is itself illegal even if you don't trade — tipper can be liable for tippee's profits.
10b5-1 plans are not a blank check — plans adopted immediately before known bad news invite SEC scrutiny.
Selective disclosure happens subtly; detailed analyst Q&A before public guidance release can violate Reg FD.

entries.title: FINRA — Financial Industry Regulatory Authority
entries.subtitle: Self-regulatory organization overseeing broker-dealers: suitability, PDT rule, margin requirements.

rules_of_thumb.label: PDT minimum equity
rules_of_thumb.note: Required if you execute 4+ day trades in 5 business days

rules_of_thumb.label: Maintenance margin floor
rules_of_thumb.note: FINRA minimum; most brokers enforce 30%

rules_of_thumb.label: Day trading buying power
rules_of_thumb.note: Available to PDT accounts with $25K+ equity

pitfalls: PDT window is rolling 5 business days, not a calendar week — Monday doesn't reset the counter.
Selling in a taxable account and rebuying in an IRA within 30 days triggers a wash sale that permanently disallows the loss.
Good faith violations in cash accounts (trading on unsettled funds) result in 90-day restrictions.

entries.title: CFTC — Commodity Futures Trading Commission
entries.subtitle: Federal regulator of futures, options on futures, and OTC derivatives: position limits, swap reporting, COT data.

rules_of_thumb.label: COT report lag
rules_of_thumb.note: Published Friday for Tuesday positioning data

rules_of_thumb.label: Margin type
rules_of_thumb.note: Futures margin is a good-faith deposit, not borrowed money

rules_of_thumb.label: Swap clearing threshold
rules_of_thumb.note: Standardized swaps must clear through a CCP

pitfalls: Physical commodity production does not automatically allow delivery against financial futures — basis risk and contract specs differ.
Spoofing (placing orders with intent to cancel) is a criminal offense under Dodd-Frank, even if no trade executes.
Hedge exemptions from position limits require demonstrable underlying business exposure — claiming them without it is an enforcement priority.

entries.title: Federal Reserve Board — The Fed
entries.subtitle: U.S. central bank: monetary policy, bank regulation, Reg T margin rules, stress testing.

rules_of_thumb.label: FOMC meetings per year
rules_of_thumb.note: Plus potential emergency meetings; each one can reprice the entire market

rules_of_thumb.label: Reg T initial margin
rules_of_thumb.note: Maximum 2:1 leverage on equity purchases in margin accounts

rules_of_thumb.label: Fed dot plot cadence
rules_of_thumb.note: Published quarterly with FOMC projections; markets trade off the median dot

pitfalls: Zero interest rates create hidden fragility (SVB duration mismatch) that only becomes visible when rates rise.
Stocks and bonds can fall simultaneously in supply-driven inflation — the Fed's dual mandate creates a dilemma.
Overreliance on 'the Fed will rescue us' is moral hazard — not every stress event triggers Fed intervention.

external_links.title: Regulation T — Extensions of Credit by Brokers and Dealers

entries.title: OCC — Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
entries.subtitle: Charters and supervises nationally chartered banks (N.A.); oversees bank derivatives activities.
aliases: office of the comptroller of the currency

rules_of_thumb.label: OCC scope
rules_of_thumb.note: Nationally chartered (N.A.) banks — JPMorgan, Citi, BofA, Wells

rules_of_thumb.label: Loan-to-one-borrower limit
rules_of_thumb.note: 25% with qualifying collateral; a common enforcement finding

rules_of_thumb.label: Derivatives report cadence
rules_of_thumb.note: OCC publishes notional and credit exposure data for top bank dealers

pitfalls: A major OCC consent order signals operational risk problems and management distraction — can suppress bank stock performance for years.
Third-party risk: banks cannot outsource their regulatory obligations — vendor failures become the bank's regulatory problem.
CRA rating below 'Satisfactory' can block acquisitions and branch expansions — a silent constraint on bank growth strategy.

entries.title: IRS Wash Sale Rule
entries.subtitle: 30-day window around a loss sale that disallows the deduction if you repurchase the same security.

rules_of_thumb.label: Restriction window
rules_of_thumb.note: 30 days before + sale date + 30 days after

rules_of_thumb.label: Disallowed loss fate
rules_of_thumb.value: Added to cost basis of replacement shares
rules_of_thumb.note: Loss is deferred, not permanently lost (except IRA repurchase)

rules_of_thumb.label: Crypto status (2025)
rules_of_thumb.value: Not covered by wash sale rule
rules_of_thumb.note: IRS has not extended wash sale to crypto — losses can be harvested and immediately repurchased

pitfalls: IRA repurchase triggers a permanent loss disallowance, not just deferral — selling at a loss in taxable and rebuying in an IRA within 30 days is especially costly.
Broker wash sale reporting is per-account only — cross-account wash sales (brokerage + IRA) are your responsibility to track.
December losses with a 30-day look-forward carry into January of the next tax year — year-end tax-loss harvesting requires careful scheduling.

external_links.title: Publication 550 — Investment Income and Expenses (Wash Sales)

entries.title: Pattern Day Trader Rule
entries.subtitle: FINRA Rule 4210: 4+ day trades in 5 business days requires $25,000 minimum equity in margin accounts.

rules_of_thumb.label: Minimum equity
rules_of_thumb.note: Must be maintained at all times once flagged as PDT

rules_of_thumb.label: Day trading buying power
rules_of_thumb.note: Intraday only; overnight positions revert to Reg T (2×)

rules_of_thumb.label: Restriction on underfunded call
rules_of_thumb.note: If margin call is not met in 5 business days

pitfalls: The 5-day window is rolling, not weekly — Monday does not reset the counter.
Depositing to meet $25K and then withdrawing back below it within 5 business days triggers a 90-day restriction.
Options day trades count toward the PDT limit exactly the same as stock day trades.

external_links.title: Day-Trading Margin Requirements: Know the Rules
Margin: Borrowing Money to Pay for Stocks

entries.title: Regulation T — Federal Reserve Margin Requirements
entries.subtitle: Sets the 50% initial margin requirement for equity purchases — the legal limit on stock-buying leverage.

rules_of_thumb.label: Initial margin (equities)
rules_of_thumb.note: Maximum 2:1 leverage on initial equity purchase

rules_of_thumb.label: Reg T call resolution
rules_of_thumb.note: Time to deposit funds after a Reg T deficiency

rules_of_thumb.label: Portfolio margin threshold
rules_of_thumb.note: Risk-based margin available above this level; typically allows ~6:1 for hedged books

pitfalls: Maximum Reg T leverage (2:1) means a 50% adverse move wipes out all equity — maintenance margin calls arrive much earlier at ~25–30%.
Day trade margin (4×) reverts to Reg T (2×) if any position is held overnight — many traders are surprised by margin calls on next-day open.
Margin interest compounds daily and is a real drag on long-held leveraged positions even without price movement.

external_links.title: Regulation T — Extensions of Credit by Brokers and Dealers

entries.title: Regulation FD — Fair Disclosure
entries.subtitle: SEC rule prohibiting selective disclosure of material non-public information to select investors.

rules_of_thumb.label: Intentional disclosure
rules_of_thumb.note: Same-day 8-K or press release when any material info is shared selectively

rules_of_thumb.label: Unintentional disclosure
rules_of_thumb.note: Before market open next day if discovered after 4pm

rules_of_thumb.label: Social media validity
rules_of_thumb.note: Posts satisfy Reg FD if the channel is disclosed as an investor communications channel

pitfalls: Off-the-record guidance to analysts is not protected — the SEC applies an objective materiality standard regardless of the speaker's intent.
Expert network channel checks become illegal if the expert shares confidential company information rather than industry knowledge.
Due diligence under NDA is a Reg FD carve-out, but the NDA must be in place before any material disclosure.

external_links.title: Compliance and Disclosure Interpretations: Regulation FD

entries.title: Insider Trading Rules
entries.subtitle: Trading on material non-public information is illegal under Rule 10b-5; includes tipping, misappropriation, and congressional trading.

rules_of_thumb.label: Criminal penalty (max)
rules_of_thumb.note: Criminal insider trading charges brought by DOJ; SEC handles civil side

rules_of_thumb.label: STOCK Act disclosure deadline
rules_of_thumb.note: Congressional members must disclose trades over $1,000 within 45 days

rules_of_thumb.label: 10b5-1 cooling-off period
rules_of_thumb.note: Required between plan adoption and first trade for officers/directors (2023 SEC rule)

pitfalls: Tipping MNPI without trading is still illegal — the tipper is liable for the tippee's profits if a personal benefit was received.
'I didn't know it was material' is not a defense — materiality is judged by an objective reasonable investor standard.
Unusual options activity before material announcements is the primary SEC surveillance signal — statistical anomalies trigger investigations before human intelligence does.
